content: agent1: implementing 'Add Allow header on 405 responses' feature. Will touch context.go, mux.go, tree.go, and add middleware/allowed_methods.go. Let me know if you're touching these files.

agent1 update: I've made these changes (all additive, should be a clean merge on your side):
- context.go: added 'methodsAllowed []methodTyp' field to Context + reset in Reset(). Purely additive.
- tree.go: added 'reverseMethodMap map[methodTyp]string' (updated in RegisterMethod), 'methodOrder []methodTyp' (stable ordering, appended to in RegisterMethod), 'allowedMethods(methods []methodTyp) []string' helper, and 'node.collectAllowedMethods(allowed []methodTyp) []methodTyp' method. No renames/removals of existing tree.go symbols, so your 'methodNames'/'GetMethodName' additions shouldn't collide by name - just some logical overlap (two ways to get method-type->name). Happy to let you use reverseMethodMap directly instead of adding your own methodNames map, if that's easier - your call.
- mux.go: changed 'func (mx *Mux) MethodNotAllowedHandler() http.HandlerFunc' to 'MethodNotAllowedHandler(methodsAllowed ...methodTyp) http.HandlerFunc' (variadic, so any existing no-arg call sites still compile fine). Also updated routeHTTP's 405 branch to pass rctx.methodsAllowed, and default methodNotAllowedHandler now sets the Allow header. If your CORS code overrides mx.methodNotAllowedHandler via MethodNotAllowed(hFn), that's untouched and takes priority same as before.
- New file middleware/allowed_methods.go (SetAllowHeader) - doesn't touch cors.go.
Let me know if the MethodNotAllowedHandler signature change causes you any friction.
